LinkedIn established that scraping publicly available data does not violate the CFAA; (2) In the EU, rules vary by country with GDPR adding a layer requiring compliance around personal data even when publicly accessible, and courts have split on whether click-wrap and browse-wrap terms of service are enforceable; (3) Other jurisdictions like Australia (Spam Act 2003 for email harvesting), Ireland (click-wrap agreements binding), and India (IT Act 2000 plus contract law) each add their own legal frameworks, making web scraping a globally inconsistent legal landscape that depends heavily on the target site's jurisdiction, terms of service, and the nature of the data collected."
